127982.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas believes that quality, (administrative) experience, and reliability must take precedence in the council. That is decisive, not a number.

127983.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas values the involvement of residents. Thinking along and participation should be widely possible, but we are cautious about referenda as an instrument. Careful decision-making via the elected municipal council and good participation are preferred.

127986.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas appreciates farmers and their entrepreneurship. Secondary activities can help maintain a healthy business but are not always suitable everywhere. The municipality must carefully weigh each case, with attention to surroundings, livability, and spatial planning.

127988.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas is cautious about new distribution centers but does not block them. The municipality must manage critically and only permit establishment if it fits with livability, sustainability, and employment in Zuidplas.

127989.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas believes employers are responsible for good housing of seasonal workers. Housing on their own farm is an option, but a central location with good facilities, supervision, and management is also a good option. Quality remains paramount.

127991.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas believes that the use of fireplaces and wood stoves can remain allowed, provided it is responsible.

127993.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas finds organizing a collective fireworks display on New Year's Eve a good alternative to private fireworks. The municipality should facilitate this, possibly also financially if necessary.

127994.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas disagrees with free bulky waste collection at home. Waste collection costs money and must be efficient. Targeted collection, possibly for a fee, is better so that costs are distributed fairly.

127995.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas believes noise standards at festivals and parties should be stricter when needed to limit nuisance for local residents. However, it must first be investigated whether current standards already provide sufficient protection before extra measures are taken.

127997.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas believes farmers can sometimes build houses on their own land. This depends, as with every applicant, on location, housing program, and spatial planning, so it fits with policy, living environment, and landscape.

127998.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas finds it important that everyone can participate in sports. For people receiving social assistance benefits, the municipality can pay for sports club memberships to remove barriers to participation and promote health and social engagement.

128001.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas disagrees with subsidies for all primary school children. A better option is targeted support for children who need it.

128003.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas opposes cuts to monument care. Historical buildings and cultural heritage are important for identity, the community, and the living environment. Investing in preservation therefore remains necessary.

128005.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas finds safety very important. More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) can contribute to supervision, compliance with rules, and a safe living environment for residents and entrepreneurs.

128006.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas believes that camera surveillance can contribute to safety but should not be expanded everywhere without reason. Its use must be targeted based on a direct cause, with clear limits on viewing area and duration as conditions.

128011.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas does not take a standard position for or against living in holiday homes. However, tailored solutions should be possible to transform recreational parks without recreational potential into regular residential areas, provided the homes comply with the Building Code and public space guidelines.

128012.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas believes new construction outside village boundaries should be possible. We want housing for our residents, in villages, green areas, and with good facilities. Construction outside villages is possible if accessibility is good.

128013.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas believes people on social assistance can do socially meaningful work, for example as volunteers. This stimulates societal involvement and allows skills development while something is expected in return.

128014.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas disagrees with stopping the exemption of municipal taxes. For residents near the poverty line, exemptions can provide financial relief and perspective, enabling them to get out of debt and continue to participate in society.

128017.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas believes more charging stations for electric cars can be added, but always balanced with 'regular' parking spaces. Combined spots can be a good solution to promote sustainable transport without increasing parking pressure.

128019.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas holds that people needing care have the right to loving, involved care that aligns with their faith, culture, or beliefs. This ensures care remains person-centered, respectful, and mindful of individual identity.

128023.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas does not oppose expanding business parks, despite limited available space. Projects like the construction of Cortelande also create new business areas to support entrepreneurship and employment.

128027.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas opposes solar panels on agricultural land. We focus on using rooftops for solar energy to preserve valuable fertile land for agricultural production.

128028.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas disagrees with general funding for school swimming. Parents have their own responsibility and can contribute themselves. The municipality can provide targeted support to families in need so all children learn to swim safely.

128029.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas opposes establishing a coffeeshop. We value safety, health, and livability and do not want places where soft drugs are freely available within the municipality.

128030.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas advocates village living. Building higher may occasionally be acceptable to realize social rental housing, but integration into the environment and connection to village atmosphere remain very important.

128031.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas believes renting out individual rooms does not have to be banned by definition. However, it must involve safe, well-organized living arrangements that fit the residential environment.

128033.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas thinks there are currently enough parking spaces in the centers and this balance must always be maintained. For housing developments, sometimes fewer parking spots than the current high standards may suffice. Investing in good public transport and cycling options is important.

128034.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas notes that future asylum seeker reception is already arranged through the realization of the ROL. This ensures the municipality complies with the Distribution Act, so an extra spread across all villages is not actually necessary.

128035.explanation: ChristenUnie/SGP Zuidplas disagrees with additional social housing in Cortelande. We adhere to the agreed 30 percent to maintain balance between housing types and living environment.

127982.explanation: VVD Zuidplas believes that when choosing a college, knowledge, experience, and administrative qualities should come first.

127983.explanation: VVD Zuidplas values good participation: residents should be involved earlier, more often, and better. A referendum is too black-and-white and lacks the meaningful dialogue needed for careful decisions.

127986.explanation: VVD Zuidplas believes that initiatives from farmers or others deserve space within the law. The municipality should be cooperative, supportive, and provide short lines of communication. Fast, clear procedures enable entrepreneurship, lively villages, and a strong local economy.

127988.explanation: VVD Zuidplas sees new business activity as an opportunity for jobs and the economy but also values the living environment and spatial quality. Every application is carefully assessed on infrastructure, traffic, environment, and impact on residents to keep business and villages in balance.

127989.explanation: VVD Zuidplas holds employers in agriculture and horticulture responsible for safe, healthy, and decent housing for seasonal workers. This does not have to be on their own premises but must meet all legal requirements and standards, possibly in cooperation with others.

127991.explanation: VVD Zuidplas believes residents should choose how to heat their homes. The municipality encourages sustainable choices but does not impose obligations. Fireplaces or wood stoves are allowed within existing safety and environmental rules.

127993.explanation: VVD Zuidplas believes initiative lies with residents, associations, and businesses. The municipality supports with clear rules, permits, safety, and enforcement so local initiative remains engaged and creative without large costs or organizational responsibility for the municipality.

127994.explanation: VVD Zuidplas opposes free bulky waste collection at home. The polluter pays. Zuidplas must stay clean, but efficiency and affordability are paramount. Free pickup increases costs and reduces residents’ responsibility.

127995.explanation: VVD Zuidplas wants events to flourish with attention to safety and livability but without unnecessary extra rules that make organizing difficult. This keeps Zuidplas lively and welcoming for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors.

127997.explanation: VVD Zuidplas supports farmers in building homes on their land within clear frameworks. Since agricultural land gets another designation, careful consideration and compliance with zoning plans and laws is essential.

127998.explanation: VVD Zuidplas believes sports must be accessible. Stimulating sports is an important municipal task. Financial support remains for children who otherwise cannot afford it. For adults on social assistance, the municipality facilitates where needed but does not take on structural costs.

128001.explanation: VVD Zuidplas values music education but believes the municipality does not have to pay for it completely. Parents and schools can make choices themselves or collaborate with external parties within the available budget.

128003.explanation: VVD Zuidplas insists the municipality must handle public funds carefully and only preserve monuments where necessary. An exception is war monuments. For other monuments, responsibility lies with owners or social organizations, aligned with efficient policy.

128005.explanation: VVD Zuidplas sees extra municipal enforcement officers as vital for a safe public space. They address nuisance and traffic offenders, work locally with community officers and residents, and use technology. Standard: one enforcement officer or community police officer per 5,000 residents.

128006.explanation: VVD Zuidplas regards camera surveillance as a tool for safety and livability aimed at busy or vulnerable spots. It is combined with enforcement officers, community police, and neighborhood watch, with attention to privacy and legal frameworks for effective supervision and prevention.

128011.explanation: VVD Zuidplas believes vacation homes are mainly for temporary stays. Permanent residence often does not meet facilities, safety, and livability requirements and can increase pressure on infrastructure and surroundings.

128012.explanation: VVD Zuidplas feels new construction outside village boundaries is sometimes necessary for starters, families, and seniors, but only if spatially responsible, infrastructure is adequate, and sufficient greenery and amenities exist to preserve livability.

128013.explanation: VVD Zuidplas believes everyone should contribute to society with support if possible, for example through volunteer work or neighborhood activities. The aim is to actively engage residents, support their independence, and strengthen social involvement, not to punish.

128014.explanation: VVD Zuidplas argues everyone should fairly contribute to local provisions. Exemptions on taxes reduce funds for core tasks. By not granting exemptions, the municipal budget can be used more effectively and purposefully.

128017.explanation: VVD Zuidplas thinks demand for charging points must come from society. The municipality facilitates quick permits and simple procedures so residents, businesses, and organizations can realize their initiatives swiftly.

128019.explanation: VVD Zuidplas finds a neighborhood-based, integrated care approach more important than individual preferences. With well-coordinated providers and a single point of contact, problems are detected early, unnecessary care avoided, and cooperation ensured for higher quality and lower costs.

128023.explanation: VVD Zuidplas supports expansion of business parks to give entrepreneurs space and strengthen the local economy and employment. Growth occurs carefully within spatial frameworks, protecting landscape, traffic, and residential environment for a healthy balance.

128027.explanation: VVD Zuidplas permits solar panels on agricultural land provided CO₂ reduction, support, and spatial policies are respected. Residents and farmers make their own choices; the municipality facilitates. Innovation is encouraged, and landscape and living environment are preserved. Wind turbines are excluded.

128028.explanation: VVD Zuidplas believes swimming education is primarily the responsibility of parents and schools. The municipality facilitates safe infrastructure and facilities but does not contribute financially, as school swimming falls outside core responsibilities.

128029.explanation: VVD Zuidplas considers it important to preserve the village character and small scale of our centers. A coffeeshop does not fit this view and may threaten livability.

128030.explanation: VVD Zuidplas feels higher buildings are allowed to meet housing demand but must fit the village character. Environment, greenery, public space, and livability remain leading factors in higher building projects.

128031.explanation: VVD Zuidplas believes the municipality should prevent landlords mainly renting out individual rooms. This reduces the availability of fully-fledged homes. The demand for rooms is better addressed by building compact and affordable housing without pressure on the regular housing stock.

128033.explanation: Adequate parking is important for accessibility and attractiveness of village centers. The number of parking spaces will be maintained at a minimum and expanded where possible. Innovative solutions, such as parking hubs, improve space usage and livability.

128034.explanation: VVD Zuidplas opposes a shelter location in the municipality. If national obligations require this, they must be small-scale and evenly distributed, with attention to safety, integration, and transparent communication to maintain livability and public support.

128035.explanation: VVD Zuidplas believes that the current percentage of social housing in Cortelande is sufficient to maintain a balanced mix for different target groups and aligns with the plans and character of the new village.

127982.explanation: Diversity in a council is a good thing, but quality and suitability must always be leading.

127983.explanation: The CDA chooses strong local democracy through the municipal council and is therefore cautious with referendums that simplify complex decisions.

127986.explanation: The CDA wants to give farmers space for secondary activities such as a shop or campsite, provided this fits the environment and is carefully assessed by the municipality.

127988.explanation: The CDA wants to seize economic opportunities but believes the establishment of large distribution centers should be carefully weighed against the living environment and accessibility of our villages.

127989.explanation: The CDA believes employers are responsible for good housing of seasonal workers, but this must happen according to clear rules and with supervision by the municipality.

127991.explanation: The CDA wants residents to be able to use their fireplaces or wood stoves, but safety, health, and the environment always come first. We want to prevent nuisance, for example through education/awareness, addressing each other, or reports in the Neighborhood App.

127993.explanation: A fireworks show in every village would be wonderful, but unfortunately, we must be realistic; there is no financial room, so we have to make choices. A facilitating role where entrepreneurs take this up is appropriate.

127994.explanation: The collection of bulky waste should be cost-covering; through the 'polluter pays' principle, costs are distributed as fairly as possible. Those who generate less waste pay less.

127995.explanation: The CDA finds the current noise standards appropriate and opposes additional requirements that unnecessarily hinder the organization of events.

127997.explanation: The CDA wants to make it easier to build on one’s own property (for example, care homes) and give employers more space to build on their own grounds to create houses. Building on one's own land requires more care and coordination with the environment and local rules.

127998.explanation: The CDA believes sports are important for health and social participation, but participation remains primarily a personal responsibility.

128001.explanation: The CDA values music education for children. The music school receives subsidies, making music education accessible and encouraged.

128003.explanation: The CDA values the preservation of monuments, but investments must be responsible and efficient; where necessary, the municipality can set priorities and make cuts.

128005.explanation: The CDA considers safety important and wants sufficient municipal enforcement officers, focusing both on enforcement and prevention to prevent problems early.

128006.explanation: The CDA is cautious about camera surveillance because it does not always fit the village character. Therefore, a careful assessment must always be made, weighing the contribution to safety, privacy, and necessity.

128011.explanation: The CDA believes homes are primarily intended for regular living; the use of holiday homes as permanent addresses must be carefully considered per park against livability and local rules. The CDA has long advocated a future-proof vision for holiday parks.

128012.explanation: The CDA wants to build where necessary but prefers new construction within existing village boundaries to maintain livable, strong villages and open space.

128013.explanation: The CDA believes people receiving social assistance can actively contribute to society, for example through socially meaningful work, because this is good for themselves and the community, as long as it is organized fairly and feasibly.

128014.explanation: The CDA believes municipal taxes should be fair and just; exemptions for people with low incomes can be made in exceptional cases but should not be standard.

128017.explanation: Current policy currently provides sufficient space to create charging stations. The CDA calls for a balanced approach, carefully considering usage before converting locations.

128019.explanation: The CDA values that people who need care have as much choice as possible for caregivers that match their faith, culture, or beliefs, especially when it involves broader needs, but this is not always feasible.

128023.explanation: With the development of Cortelande, the area of business parks increases; further expansion is undesirable.

128027.explanation: Solar panels belong on roofs; fertile agricultural land must be avoided. We choose sustainable energy but protect our valuable landscape and food production. Creative solutions such as solar panels on noise barriers or business roofs are preferred.

128028.explanation: The CDA wants to support and facilitate good swimming pools so every child can obtain a swimming diploma in an accessible way and no child is left behind. Together we ensure swimming safety remains accessible to everyone; school swimming is not the most effective way for this.

128029.explanation: The CDA does not want a coffeeshop in Zuidplas. This does not fit the village character of our municipality. We choose a healthy and safe living environment where young people encounter soft drugs as little as possible.

128030.explanation: The CDA prefers building that fits the village character of Zuidplas. Tall apartment buildings usually do not belong there. In connection with the surroundings, more is possible in Nieuwerkerk aan den IJssel than in other villages. The CDA mainly wants low-rise buildings and homes that fit the green, recognizable character.

128031.explanation: Homes are primarily for families and households; room rental can cause nuisance and does not fit the village character. At the same time, we do not want rigid barriers: flexibility should be possible for temporary residents within clear rules.

128033.explanation: Parking spaces are needed to maintain livability in the centers. The CDA wants sufficient parking facilities to prevent nuisance and annoyance. We look per center at what is needed and feasible, with attention to daily practice.

128034.explanation: The CDA prefers small-scale shelter, fitting the village character and with attention to connection and support in each center. In each situation, a careful assessment will be made of what is appropriate, considering distribution, location suitability, and available facilities.

128035.explanation: The current principle of 30 percent social housing in Cortelande is appropriate. Building more social housing can disturb the balance and cause higher costs. The CDA chooses a good mix that fits livability for all target groups.

127982.explanation: A good balance of men and women provides different ideas and experiences. That helps to make better decisions. Quality is always paramount, but equal opportunities are part of that.

127983.explanation: Residents must have influence on important decisions. That can be done through participation, discussions, and sometimes a citizen's council. A referendum can help but is not always the best form.

127986.explanation: Farmers must be given space to earn extra income, but not everything fits everywhere. The municipality must assess what is possible per location and take the environment and landscape into account.

127988.explanation: Depending on spatial integration, it must remain possible to establish distribution centers in Zuidplas. This is important for local employment. We prefer not a landscape of big boxes but creative spatial integration.

127989.explanation: Employers must take responsibility for good housing. That prevents nuisance and ensures safe, decent living places for employees.

127991.explanation: Wood burning can cause health nuisance. We want to raise awareness among residents and limit nuisance without banning everything immediately.

127993.explanation: In surrounding municipalities, fireworks shows are already organized. In our villages, there is little enthusiasm for this. We prefer safety and peace and leave room for residents to celebrate New Year's Eve in their own way.

127995.explanation: Small-scale festivals and parties must remain possible. Events provide liveliness, but noise nuisance must be limited. A good balance between fun and livability is important.

127997.explanation: Realizing houses on agricultural land is considered a good development by D66, especially if the initiative comes from the farmers themselves. Spatial integration is a condition for this.

127998.explanation: Sports are important for health and social interaction. People with low incomes must also be able to participate. D66 Zuidplas believes that money should not be a barrier.

128001.explanation: Music helps children develop and learn to collaborate. With subsidies, every child gets this chance, regardless of their parents' income.

128003.explanation: Monuments tell the story of our villages and give Zuidplas its character. They are important for identity, tourism, and livability. Budget cuts to monument care mean irreversible loss.

128005.explanation: Visible enforcement officers ensure peace and safety. They are approachable and know the neighborhood. This helps to identify problems early and prevents escalation.

128006.explanation: Cameras can contribute to safety in places where necessary. They assist with prevention and investigation. We deploy them selectively and always with attention to privacy.

128011.explanation: In some cases, permanent residence can be a solution. This can help with housing shortages but must always have clear rules to prevent abuse. To live permanently, holiday homes must comply with building regulations (such as fire safety).

128012.explanation: We prefer building within existing villages (densification and adding floors), preserving the open landscape. Exceptions should allow building outside village boundaries, always aiming for at least 60 percent affordable housing.

128013.explanation: Participating in society is important. For those who can, socially useful work is a good way to stay active, always with tailored support and guidance.

128014.explanation: The municipality has a balanced package to support people with low incomes. We therefore do not support stopping the waiving of municipal taxes for residents with low incomes.

128017.explanation: More charging stations make electric driving easier. The municipality must actively manage this, considering enough 'normal' (non-electric) parking spots.

128019.explanation: Of course, there must be room for people to choose based on faith, culture, or belief system. However, quality of care must be the central focus.

128023.explanation: Employment is important. Expansion must be sustainable and fit the environment. Quality takes precedence over quantity.

128027.explanation: Sustainable energy is necessary. Solar panels on agricultural land can be installed if done carefully and fitting the landscape. Proper integration is important.

128028.explanation: Swimming skills are important for safety. Children's swimming ability is declining. The municipality can support up to a certain extent, but schools and parents also play an important role.

128029.explanation: This requires careful consideration. We look at safety, health, and nuisance. No quick yes or no.

128030.explanation: Stacked housing is suitable in some locations, such as around the station in Nieuwerkerk. It results in affordable homes but must always fit the village and consider quality of living.

128031.explanation: Subdividing homes into multiple units can add housing but may also cause nuisance. Clear rules and tailored solutions are needed to keep neighborhoods livable.

128033.explanation: D66 Zuidplas wants to reduce dependence on cars. We value playing and cycling near homes, so we combine parking with good public transport, cycling, and shared mobility.

128034.explanation: We prefer the current shelter, which belongs to the existing allocation and planned regional location, to be in one place. This way, good facilities and safety are easier to arrange. If extra tasks arise (from the national government), shelter must be fairly distributed across all villages in Zuidplas.

128035.explanation: A diverse housing offer is important for the development of Cortelande. This ensures livability and social cohesion. Current plans include much space for affordable housing: 60 percent affordable, of which 30 percent social rent. Cortelande will thus have more social housing than other villages.

127982.explanation: Quality prevails; it could easily become four female alderwomen.

127986.explanation: Depends on the situation, for example, the number of camping pitches.

127988.explanation: NEZ is against large box-type buildings but may allow them depending on scale.

127989.explanation: Not only housing near employers. NEZ supports decent housing.

127993.explanation: But this may also be an alternative (light/droneshow).

127997.explanation: If the zoning plan is changed to residential use, farmers must have the opportunity to self-realize on their own land. If farming activities are restricted, it must be considered whether they can use their land in another way.

127998.explanation: Generous policy regarding special social assistance, including with respect to swimming lessons.

128001.explanation: Individual or classroom instruction should be encouraged.

128006.explanation: But only if there is cause for it.

128011.explanation: Depends on the recreation park and the possibility to apply dual zoning.

128012.explanation: Provided the scale fits the rural, village character.

128013.explanation: If this is a possibility for the person, it should be pursued.

128027.explanation: But first solar panels on roofs.

128028.explanation: NEZ strongly supports learning to swim at an early age. School swimming is one way to achieve this.

128029.explanation: Surrounding municipalities offer sufficient facilities for those who need them.

128030.explanation: NEZ stands for the village character. Stacked buildings may not exceed four, in extreme cases eight floors. This all depends on the location. However, this should be an exception.

128033.explanation: If there is demand for it.

128034.explanation: NEZ advocates for a distribution of status holders across the villages.

128035.explanation: Thirty percent social housing is currently a good goal.

127982.explanation: Although we would like to see more women in the council, we do not agree with this statement. The residents of Zuidplas choose who they want to see in the municipal council.

127983.explanation: We welcome participation processes, but not a referendum. This delays processes.

127986.explanation: Farmers must be given the opportunity to open a shop or campsite. This strengthens the local economy and short supply chains, provides farmers with extra income, and at the same time preserves the living environment with clear rules.

127988.explanation: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers. This way we protect residential and living environments, limit traffic and CO₂ nuisance, and create space for sustainable and small-scale business activity.

127989.explanation: Employers should be responsible for housing seasonal workers, but this is often undesirable when it must take place on their own premises. It is important to monitor the quality of modest housing.

127991.explanation: No immediate total ban, but steering toward a phase-out, rules in case of nuisance, and protection of nearby residents.

127993.explanation: A central fireworks show is safer and cleaner than individual fireworks and prevents much nuisance and injury. At the same time, it offers a moment for all residents to start the new year together. This fits a social and responsible municipality.

127994.explanation: Free bulky waste collection at home leads to more waste and higher costs for the entire community. A better system stimulates reuse and separation and collects waste in a targeted manner under clear conditions, with consideration for residents who truly need it.

127995.explanation: Stricter noise standards protect residents from nuisance and health damage, while still allowing events. Celebrating is fine, but not at the expense of livability in residential neighborhoods.

127997.explanation: Good arable land is important in Zuidplas and we must be sparing with it. It is not a problem, for example, to build a home for parents passing their business to their children.

127998.explanation: We want to support families who have little to no financial means to have their children join a sports club or learn to swim.

128001.explanation: Music education contributes to development, self-confidence, and equal opportunities. With municipal subsidies, we ensure music lessons are accessible to all primary school children regardless of their parents' income.

128003.explanation: The municipality must not cut back on monument care. This way cultural heritage and historic buildings remain preserved for current and future generations.

128005.explanation: More municipal enforcement officers are needed in Zuidplas. This will improve safety, limit nuisance, and provide support to residents, focusing on prevention and citizen-oriented enforcement.

128006.explanation: There should be no more camera surveillance in Zuidplas. Safety is mainly improved through prevention and enforcement by people, not through additional cameras that infringe on privacy.

128011.explanation: The municipality should allow holiday homes to be used as permanent residences. This increases available housing, helping to solve housing shortages and preventing homes from standing empty. It also offers flexibility for residents.

128012.explanation: The housing shortage calls for construction, even if that is partly outside existing village boundaries. However, this must be done carefully, fitting the landscape and maintaining facilities and accessibility. Rigidly sticking to boundaries does not solve the shortage.

128013.explanation: People receiving welfare benefits should not be forced to do social work. Welfare should provide support and create opportunities, not punish or obligate. Investing in guidance, education, and individual pathways is more effective.

128014.explanation: We do not want to burden residents who can least afford it. We want to spare residents with low incomes. Remission prevents debts, increases security of existence, and aligns with our commitment to combating poverty and promoting equal opportunities.

128017.explanation: The energy transition requires concrete facilities. More charging points accelerate clean transport, reduce CO₂ emissions, and make electric driving accessible to everyone, including those without a private driveway. This fits our commitment to sustainability and future-proof mobility.

128019.explanation: Good care is appropriate care. Alignment with faith, culture, or beliefs increases trust and effectiveness. Within public frameworks and affordability, freedom of choice must be central, so everyone can receive full and dignified care.

128023.explanation: Economic development is important, but not at any cost. Expansion is possible only if it is sustainable, fits the environment, and contributes to local employment. Priority for circular, clean businesses and smart use of space.

128027.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce and valuable. We do not want to sacrifice it for solar fields. Solar panels should primarily be placed on roofs and alongside infrastructure such as the A12 and A20 highways. This way we combine the energy transition with protection of the landscape and food production.

128028.explanation: Swimming skills are not a luxury but basic safety. School swimming increases equal opportunities, prevents accidents, and relieves low-income families. Investing upfront is social, preventive, and ultimately cheaper than repairing afterward.

128029.explanation: Tolerated sales under clear rules increase safety and manageability. A coffeeshop removes soft drugs from the illegal circuit, reduces street dealers, and gives the municipality control over enforcement, public health, and livability. Regulation is better than looking away.

128030.explanation: To address the housing shortage, densification is necessary. Medium-high to higher residential flats in suitable locations provide space for affordable housing without consuming extra open space. The condition is good integration, accessibility, and quality of living.

128031.explanation: A general ban goes too far. Room rental can be necessary for students and singles. The municipality must steer quality, safety, and prevent nuisance, and intervene where abuses occur.

128033.explanation: More parking spaces do not solve structural problems and reduce livability and space for greenery. We choose safe village centers with more room for walking, cycling, and public transport. Smart parking and shared mobility are more effective than more asphalt.

128034.explanation: When there are follow-up requests to accommodate asylum seekers, it is important that every village is involved.

128035.explanation: More social housing must be built in Cortelande to provide a place for people with lower incomes, starters, and the elderly. This way the new village becomes mixed, affordable, and social, instead of a neighborhood for a small group.

127982.explanation: It would be nice if the executive board also reflected society. But the qualities of a candidate alderman are not related to gender.

127983.explanation: Topics with major social impact deserve democratic participation through a corrective referendum.

127986.explanation: Provided the location lends itself to this.

127988.explanation: Depending on the location and the traffic pressure, which is already far too high.

127989.explanation: The law forbids this for good reason. It opens the door to exploitation of people and poor living conditions.

127991.explanation: However, residents must use the burn alert consciously.

127993.explanation: When consumer fireworks disappear, it is good to offer an alternative, but this can also be done with light, drones, or other effects.

127994.explanation: This would significantly increase waste costs, but charging a fair fee would make it worthwhile to offer for those who cannot go to the site themselves.

127995.explanation: When these are near nature, it is important for the environment.

127997.explanation: There are clear laws and regulations for this, where the municipality helps determine how many and what type of homes may be developed on a location.

127998.explanation: Sports and fully participating in society is an important step in participation. There should be limits on membership costs; associations can also offer discounts here.

128001.explanation: Students whose parents are financially capable do not need this extra support, allowing more to be done with the funds that are available.

128003.explanation: There are subsidies for this. We must preserve our heritage.

128005.explanation: The visibility of enforcement officers greatly influences residents’ sense of safety. Visibility of community officers increases residents’ security feelings. It also increases the chance of being caught, making rules more than just suggestions.

128006.explanation: The safety of residents and entrepreneurs is a greater good than the privacy of troublemakers.

128011.explanation: In the current housing crisis, it is incomprehensible that people cannot live on a recreational park, but it is wise to make this temporary. Starting with a 10-year period, so the prices of recreational homes will not equal those of regular homes.

128012.explanation: It is better to build on the edges of villages and perhaps slightly beyond than to fully build up the existing centers. Existing village centers are becoming paved over, which harms residents’ health and causes heat stress. All residents deserve to live near green spaces.

128013.explanation: Participating to the best of one’s ability and meaningful daytime activities are important, but mandatory work in exchange for benefits is undesirable and leads to negative competition and wage disputes.

128014.explanation: There must always be a solid safety net for people in poverty.

128017.explanation: The possibility of applying for electric charging points should be simplified.

128019.explanation: To keep care affordable and accessible, procurement must be logical. Residents who want to use other care providers due to their religious beliefs should pay a reasonable contribution themselves.

128023.explanation: This should be considered per business park and always depends on location and surroundings.

128027.explanation: Allow, not require. Also, the landscape impact should be considered per location.

128028.explanation: By collectively reinstating school swimming, it also becomes cheaper for parents who currently have to pay privately. Additionally, it is financially beneficial for swimming pools.

128029.explanation: Provided a suitable location becomes available, this is better than the illegal drug trade currently taking place in our villages.

128030.explanation: High-rise buildings are not necessarily undesirable, but must fit within the landscape. Expanding high-rise near existing high-rise should definitely be studied, with a maximum building height that suits the environment.

128031.explanation: We must protect tenants from exorbitant rents. Renting a room privately to a student is not seen as a problem.

128033.explanation: It is difficult to create more parking spaces in existing centers, and where it is possible it usually comes at the expense of green space. Therefore, it is more important to focus on accessibility by bike and public transport.

128034.explanation: Humane shelter must be paramount and large-scale shelter locations put more pressure on society than smaller ones.

128035.explanation: The needs of the home seeker must be considered, rather than just the needs of developers who want to make money. Housing is a right, not a business model.

127982.explanation: Encourage more women, more diversity, and inclusivity.

127983.explanation: Residents must be able to think along and co-decide on all subjects. We find that very important; putting residents at the center and seriously involving them in decisions affecting the living environment of Zuidplas residents.

127986.explanation: Under conditions and in consultation with the surroundings and residents.

127989.explanation: Under conditions and in consultation with the surroundings and residents.

127994.explanation: Financially accessible waste collection prevents littering on the streets.

127998.explanation: Access to sports must be open to everyone. It promotes social well-being.

128003.explanation: Cherish and preserve our historic places, buildings, and unique landscape features.

128005.explanation: More safety on the street, at stations, and in places where residents feel unsafe.

128006.explanation: In places where residents and shopkeepers report feeling unsafe. Respecting privacy.

128011.explanation: As a possible solution to the housing shortage. It must remain the own choice of the current owners of holiday homes.

128012.explanation: The villages must retain their own identity, with sufficient space between the different centers.

128013.explanation: Participating in society is important. Naturally, according to ability.

128023.explanation: Not at the expense of nature and green infrastructure. In consultation with the immediate surroundings.

128027.explanation: Stimulate unused potential for solar panels on roofs of residents and businesses. We must be careful with our greenery and meadow birds.

128028.explanation: Swimming skills are essential and must be accessible to everyone.

128030.explanation: Low-rise buildings are the norm. Higher only in the centers, where possible.

128031.explanation: It can actually be a solution to the housing shortage.

128033.explanation: Promote more cycling and walking. Expand disabled parking spaces.

128034.explanation: Shelters should be small-scale, self-managed, without COA involvement, and only for genuine refugees.

statements.title: The new village Cortelande should include more social housing than is currently planned.
The residents of Zuidplas should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Zuidplas moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Zuidplas should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Business parks in Zuidplas should be allowed to expand.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
More tall residential apartment blocks should be allowed in Zuidplas.
The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed in Zuidplas.

titleParts.text: In Zuidplas moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages or small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Zuidplas should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Future housing for asylum seekers should be distributed across all villages of Zuidplas.

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

titleParts.text: moet worden verdeeld over alle dorpen van Zuidplas.
